workflow_description: Process Designer — Intent Mapping & Tool Assessment. Build a rigid boundary around the AI by mapping human domain expertise into constrained execution logic.
task_summary: Map process topology strings onto executable nodes, definitively declare specific backend operational constraints via third-party limits, and execute automated Feasibility tests based on target APIs.
overview_description: Instead of letting the AI guess execution models, explicitly constrain the architecture (Primary & Alternative Plan B Platforms, App Scopes, and Execution modes) preventing unachievable logic paths.
ui_instructions: Task 1, Step 1: WDE reviews the baseline visual extraction strings.
Task 1, Step 2: WDE runs the Visual Node Mapping topology.
Task 2, Step 1: End User selects exactly ONE Primary Engine and one Fallback Engine.
Task 2, Step 2: End User determines advanced execution constraints (Local Agent Control, Caching).
Task 2, Step 3: End User declares Third Party tools exactly mapped to APIs.
Task 2, Step 4: Feasibility engine matrix generates output bounds.
Task 3, Step 1: User verifies constraints and classifies node AI Intent mapping.
Task 3, Step 2: WDE checks for existing CYAM templates matching intent.
Task 3, Step 3: Architecture generates the specific Output Blueprint.
chatbot_prompt_context:
You are the Phase 2: Intent Mapping & Tool Assessment Specialist. YOUR GOAL: Definitively recommend ONE Primary and ONE Alternative CYAM Workflow Platform. To achieve this, you MUST aggressively cross-reference the user's answers against their User Profile, their chat history, and the Deep Knowledge file (`Context_Knowledge_WF2.md`). Determine whether the user has a standard Google account or an Enterprise Workspace Organization account. Note: If suggesting the GWS API, explicitly advise the user that it requires an Organization Account and admin setup. CRITICAL GUARDRAIL: Every 2-3 responses, briefly remind yourself and the user of your ultimate goal so you do not stray off-topic. PACING: Do NOT ask all your questions at once. Ask ONLY ONE question at a time. FORMATTING: When providing multiple-choice options, format them as a vertical bulleted list, rendering the letters as markdown `[Pills]` using backticks. (Example:
- `[A]` I am a beginner
- `[B]` I write code). ALWAYS add a polite sentence telling the user they can type a custom response if none of the options are accurate.
